proposed_hypotheses.text: `feature_025`-positive patients have a higher `objective_response` rate than `feature_025`-negative patients.

analyses.code: Two-proportion z-test of objective_response by feature_025.
analyses.result_summary: Response was 23.5% in feature_025-positive patients versus 16.7% in feature_025-negative patients (risk difference 6.7%).

proposed_hypotheses.text: `feature_015`-positive patients have a higher `objective_response` rate than `feature_015`-negative patients.

analyses.code: Two-proportion z-test of objective_response by feature_015.
analyses.result_summary: Response was 22.1% with feature_015=1 versus 16.5% with feature_015=0 (risk difference 5.6%).

proposed_hypotheses.text: `feature_023`-positive patients have a higher `objective_response` rate than `feature_023`-negative patients.

analyses.code: Two-proportion z-test of objective_response by feature_023.
analyses.result_summary: Response was 22.0% with feature_023=1 versus 16.6% with feature_023=0 (risk difference 5.4%).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `feature_027` at level 2 have a lower `objective_response` rate than patients with `feature_027` at levels 0 or 1.
Each one-level increase in ordinal `feature_027` is associated with a lower `objective_response` probability.

analyses.code: Two-proportion z-test comparing feature_027 level 2 with levels 0/1.
analyses.result_summary: Response was 12.9% for feature_027=2 versus 19.8% for feature_027=0/1 (risk difference -6.9%).

analyses.code: Univariate linear probability regression of objective_response on ordinal feature_027.
analyses.result_summary: Linear probability trend estimated -3.5% lower response per one SD of feature_027; per raw level coefficient was -5.2%.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients in the highest quartile of continuous `feature_004` have a lower `objective_response` rate than patients below that quartile.
Patients in the highest quartile of continuous `feature_031` have a lower `objective_response` rate than patients below that quartile.

analyses.code: Two-proportion z-test using the top quartile threshold of feature_004.
analyses.result_summary: At feature_004 >= 6.4, response was 15.7% versus 19.8% below that threshold (risk difference -4.1%).

analyses.code: Two-proportion z-test using the top quartile threshold of feature_031.
analyses.result_summary: At feature_031 >= 73.3, response was 16.6% versus 19.5% below that threshold (risk difference -2.8%).

proposed_hypotheses.text: `feature_001`-positive patients have a lower `objective_response` rate than `feature_001`-negative patients.
`feature_032`-positive patients have a lower `objective_response` rate than `feature_032`-negative patients.

analyses.code: Two-proportion z-test of objective_response by feature_001.
analyses.result_summary: Response was 16.8% with feature_001=1 versus 19.1% with feature_001=0 (risk difference -2.3%).

analyses.code: Two-proportion z-test of objective_response by feature_032.
analyses.result_summary: Response was 16.6% with feature_032=1 versus 19.0% with feature_032=0 (risk difference -2.4%).

proposed_hypotheses.text: `feature_025` and `feature_015` interact positively: the combined `feature_025=1` and `feature_015=1` subgroup has a higher `objective_response` rate than expected from adding their separate risk differences.

analyses.code: Additive interaction z-test using response-rate difference-in-differences for feature_025 and feature_015.
analyses.result_summary: Response rates for feature_025/feature_015 cells were 16.8% (0/0), 16.6% (0/1), 15.9% (1/0), and 34.9% (1/1); additive interaction was 19.3%.

proposed_hypotheses.text: `feature_025` and `feature_023` interact positively: the combined `feature_025=1` and `feature_023=1` subgroup has a higher `objective_response` rate than expected from adding their separate risk differences.

analyses.code: Additive interaction z-test using response-rate difference-in-differences for feature_025 and feature_023.
analyses.result_summary: Response rates for feature_025/feature_023 cells were 16.9% (0/0), 16.5% (0/1), 16.0% (1/0), and 34.7% (1/1); additive interaction was 19.1%.

proposed_hypotheses.text: `feature_015` and `feature_023` interact positively: the combined `feature_015=1` and `feature_023=1` subgroup has a higher `objective_response` rate than expected from adding their separate risk differences.

analyses.code: Additive interaction z-test using response-rate difference-in-differences for feature_015 and feature_023.
analyses.result_summary: Response rates for feature_015/feature_023 cells were 16.8% (0/0), 16.0% (0/1), 16.3% (1/0), and 30.8% (1/1); additive interaction was 15.3%.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients jointly positive for `feature_025`, `feature_015`, and `feature_023` have a much higher `objective_response` rate than all other patients.
The three-way interaction among `feature_025`, `feature_015`, and `feature_023` further increases `objective_response` beyond their pairwise interactions.

analyses.code: Two-proportion z-test comparing feature_025/feature_015/feature_023 triple-positive patients with all others.
analyses.result_summary: The triple-positive subgroup had response 63.3% versus 16.5% in all other patients (risk difference 46.8%).

analyses.code: Three-way additive interaction contrast over the 2x2x2 response-rate table.
analyses.result_summary: The three-way additive interaction estimate was 46.2% after accounting for all lower-order cells.

proposed_hypotheses.text: `feature_001` attenuates the favorable association of `feature_025` with `objective_response`; the `feature_025` risk difference is smaller when `feature_001=1` than when `feature_001=0`.

analyses.code: Additive interaction z-test using response-rate difference-in-differences for feature_025 and feature_001.
analyses.result_summary: For feature_025/feature_001 cells, response rates were 16.6% (0/0), 17.2% (0/1), 24.8% (1/0), and 15.8% (1/1); additive interaction was -9.6%.

proposed_hypotheses.text: `feature_032` attenuates the favorable association of `feature_025` with `objective_response`; the `feature_025` risk difference is smaller when `feature_032=1` than when `feature_032=0`.

analyses.code: Additive interaction z-test using response-rate difference-in-differences for feature_025 and feature_032.
analyses.result_summary: For feature_025/feature_032 cells, response rates were 16.7% (0/0), 16.6% (0/1), 24.2% (1/0), and 16.5% (1/1); additive interaction was -7.5%.

proposed_hypotheses.text: `feature_001` attenuates the favorable association of `feature_015` with `objective_response`; the `feature_015` risk difference is smaller when `feature_001=1` than when `feature_001=0`.

analyses.code: Additive interaction z-test using response-rate difference-in-differences for feature_015 and feature_001.
analyses.result_summary: The feature_015-by-feature_001 additive interaction was -6.0%, with lower-than-additive response in the double-positive subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: `feature_032` attenuates the favorable association of `feature_023` with `objective_response`; the `feature_023` risk difference is smaller when `feature_032=1` than when `feature_032=0`.

analyses.code: Additive interaction z-test using response-rate difference-in-differences for feature_023 and feature_032.
analyses.result_summary: The feature_023-by-feature_032 additive interaction was -6.4%, indicating weaker response gain for feature_023 among feature_032-positive patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for `feature_025`, `feature_015`, `feature_023`, `feature_001`, `feature_032`, `feature_027`, and continuous features `feature_004`, `feature_031`, `feature_059`, and `feature_053`, the positive `feature_025`-by-`feature_015` interaction remains associated with higher `objective_response`.
After the same adjustment set, the positive `feature_025`-by-`feature_023` interaction remains associated with higher `objective_response`.
After the same adjustment set, the positive `feature_015`-by-`feature_023` interaction remains associated with higher `objective_response`.

analyses.code: Robust-SE linear probability model with selected main effects and interactions.
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted linear probability coefficient for feature_025_x_feature_015 was 19.4%.

analyses.code: Robust-SE linear probability model with selected main effects and interactions.
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted linear probability coefficient for feature_025_x_feature_023 was 19.2%.

analyses.code: Robust-SE linear probability model with selected main effects and interactions.
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted linear probability coefficient for feature_015_x_feature_023 was 15.3%.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for the selected main effects and interactions, `feature_001` remains independently associated with lower `objective_response`.
After adjustment for the selected main effects and interactions, `feature_032` remains independently associated with lower `objective_response`.

analyses.code: Robust-SE linear probability model with selected main effects and interactions.
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted linear probability coefficient for feature_001 was 0.6% and was not significant, so an independent adverse main effect was not supported after interaction terms were included.

analyses.code: Robust-SE linear probability model with selected main effects and interactions.
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted linear probability coefficient for feature_032 was -0.2% and was not significant, so an independent adverse main effect was not supported after interaction terms were included.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for selected binary signals and interactions, each one-level increase in `feature_027` remains associated with lower `objective_response`.
After adjustment for selected binary signals and interactions, each one-SD increase in `feature_004` remains associated with lower `objective_response`.

analyses.code: Robust-SE linear probability model with selected main effects and interactions.
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted linear probability coefficient for feature_027_per_level was -5.3%.

analyses.code: Robust-SE linear probability model with selected main effects and interactions.
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted linear probability coefficient for feature_004_per_sd was -2.0%.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher continuous `feature_059` is associated with higher `objective_response`, comparing the highest quartile with lower values.
Higher continuous `feature_053` is associated with lower `objective_response`, comparing the highest quartile with lower values.

analyses.code: Two-proportion z-test using the top quartile threshold of feature_059.
analyses.result_summary: At feature_059 >= 4.1, response was 19.8% versus 18.3% below that threshold (risk difference 1.5%).

analyses.code: Two-proportion z-test using the top quartile threshold of feature_053.
analyses.result_summary: At feature_053 >= 24.1, response was 17.6% versus 19.1% below that threshold (risk difference -1.5%).

proposed_hypotheses.text: `feature_005`-positive patients have a higher `objective_response` rate than `feature_005`-negative patients.
`feature_074`-positive patients have a lower `objective_response` rate than `feature_074`-negative patients.

analyses.code: Two-proportion z-test of objective_response by feature_005.
analyses.result_summary: Response was 20.2% with feature_005=1 versus 18.7% with feature_005=0 (risk difference 1.6%).

analyses.code: Two-proportion z-test of objective_response by feature_074.
analyses.result_summary: Response was 17.7% with feature_074=1 versus 18.8% with feature_074=0 (risk difference -1.2%).

proposed_hypotheses.text: `feature_040` and `feature_046` interact positively: the combined `feature_040=1` and `feature_046=1` subgroup has a higher `objective_response` rate than expected from adding their separate risk differences.

analyses.code: Exploratory additive interaction z-test over all binary feature pairs.
analyses.result_summary: The feature_040-by-feature_046 additive interaction was 4.8%; the double-positive cell response was 21.2%.

proposed_hypotheses.text: `feature_050` and `feature_071` interact negatively: the combined `feature_050=1` and `feature_071=1` subgroup has a lower `objective_response` rate than expected from adding their separate risk differences.

analyses.code: Exploratory additive interaction z-test over all binary feature pairs.
analyses.result_summary: The feature_050-by-feature_071 additive interaction was -4.3%; the double-positive cell response was 15.7%.

proposed_hypotheses.text: `feature_005` and `feature_013` interact negatively: the combined `feature_005=1` and `feature_013=1` subgroup has a lower `objective_response` rate than expected from adding their separate risk differences.

analyses.code: Exploratory additive interaction z-test over all binary feature pairs.
analyses.result_summary: The feature_005-by-feature_013 additive interaction was -6.7%, but the evidence did not reach p<0.05.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The favorable association of `feature_025` with `objective_response` is weaker among patients with high `feature_004` than among patients with low `feature_004`.

analyses.code: Additive interaction z-test for feature_025 and median-split feature_004.
analyses.result_summary: The feature_025-by-high-feature_004 additive interaction was 0.6%, indicating no meaningful subgroup modification by median-split feature_004.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The favorable association of `feature_025` with `objective_response` is weaker among patients with `feature_027=2` than among patients with `feature_027=0` or `feature_027=1`.

analyses.code: Additive interaction z-test for feature_025 and feature_027 level 2.
analyses.result_summary: The feature_025-by-feature_027_level_2 additive interaction was -1.0%, indicating no statistically significant modification.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Across all binary-feature pairs with adequate cell sizes, the strongest statistically significant additive interactions with `objective_response` are concentrated in pairs involving `feature_025`, `feature_015`, `feature_023`, `feature_001`, and `feature_032`.

analyses.code: Exhaustive pairwise additive interaction screen among binary features with all 2x2 cells >= 30 patients.
analyses.result_summary: Screened 793 binary pair interactions; Bonferroni threshold was 6.31e-5. The most significant pairs were feature_015:feature_025 DID=19.3% p=0.00e+0; feature_015:feature_023 DID=15.3% p=0.00e+0; feature_001:feature_025 DID=-9.6% p=0.00e+0; feature_025:feature_023 DID=19.1% p=0.00e+0; feature_025:feature_032 DID=-7.5% p=6.70e-10; feature_015:feature_001 DID=-6.0% p=8.88e-10; feature_032:feature_023 DID=-6.4% p=2.16e-8; feature_001:feature_023 DID=-4.9% p=3.69e-7; feature_015:feature_032 DID=-4.6% p=5.97e-5; feature_040:feature_046 DID=4.8% p=2.42e-4.
